skill.name: product-naming
skill.description: Guides product, project, and module naming through discovery, constraints, naming routes, validation, and final selection.
skill.summary: A structured product naming workflow that turns vague ideas into validated brand name options.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited lines are examples in documentation. The weak cryptography and entropy findings are also false positives from Chinese frontmatter text, with no executable code, prompt injection, obfuscation, or data exfiltration found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited locations are fenced Markdown examples that show naming workflow output. They are not executable commands and contain no user input execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation, and the cited blocks contain Chinese examples and product names, not shell syntax. No executable script, interpreter directive, or command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 3, but the line is a Chinese description of the product naming workflow. No hash function, encryption API, or cryptographic operation appears at this location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is frontmatter prose, not code. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or another weak cryptographic primitive.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy Markdown Content
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged high entropy at the start of the file, but the file is readable Markdown with Chinese text and structured instructions. No binary, encrypted payload, encoded blob, or obfuscated code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows normal YAML frontmatter and human-readable Markdown. The entropy signal is likely caused by non-ASCII Chinese content, not hidden executable content.

content.value_statement: Product names often fail when teams jump straight to word lists. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a structured naming process based on positioning, constraints, routes, and validation.
actual_capabilities: Guides discovery of users, product essence, boundaries, brand tone, and naming constraints.
Extracts naming keywords, tensions, and priorities before generating name options.
Creates two to four naming routes with sample names, strengths, and tradeoffs.
Narrows a chosen route into three to five candidate names with concise rationale.
Prompts competitor naming research before recommending final naming strategy.
Summarizes the final name, slogan option, strategy, and decision basis.
limitations: Does not perform legal trademark clearance or domain purchase checks.
Requires user input about audience, positioning, tone, and naming constraints.
May need external search access for competitor research.
Focuses on naming strategy, not full brand identity design.

use_cases.title: Name a New Startup Product
use_cases.description: Turn an early product idea into naming routes and validated candidate names.

use_cases.title: Rename an Internal Tool
use_cases.description: Create a name that reflects the tool purpose, users, and team culture.

use_cases.title: Name a Software Module
use_cases.description: Find a clear module name that fits product architecture and user expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Naming Session
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a new product name.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me name a new product. Ask me the discovery questions first, then guide me through naming routes before suggesting final names.

prompt_templates.title: Explore Naming Routes
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt after basic context is available.
prompt_templates.prompt: Based on this product context, extract naming constraints and propose three distinct naming routes with example names, strengths, and risks.

prompt_templates.title: Refine a Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt after choosing a naming route.
prompt_templates.prompt: I prefer the route focused on warmth and collaboration. Generate three to five names only within that direction, with a short rationale for each.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Final Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for final decision support.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research naming patterns in this market, compare them with our shortlist, and recommend the final name, naming strategy, and optional slogan.

output_examples.input: I need a name for an async standup tool for remote developers.
output_examples.output: The skill would first ask about users, product role, boundaries, brand tone, and constraints before proposing naming routes.

output_examples.input: Our chosen direction is short, friendly, and action based.
output_examples.output: The skill would produce a focused shortlist such as action-word names, each with positioning rationale and tradeoffs.

output_examples.input: We like one candidate name but need confidence before deciding.
output_examples.output: The skill would review competitor naming patterns, recommend a name strategy, and summarize the decision basis.

best_practices: Share the target users, main use case, tone, and naming constraints before asking for candidates.
Choose one naming route before asking for a deeper shortlist.
Validate favorite names with competitor research, trademark review, and domain checks.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a long name list before explaining the product essence.
Do not evaluate names only by whether they sound pleasant.
Do not skip market research when the name will be public or commercial.

faq.question: Can this skill create names immediately?
faq.answer: It is designed to ask discovery questions first, then generate names from a clear strategy.

faq.question: Does it check trademarks?
faq.answer: No. It can suggest validation steps, but legal trademark clearance needs qualified review.

faq.question: Can it work for Chinese and English names?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow supports language preferences and can compare single-brand or bilingual naming strategies.

faq.question: Does it need web access?
faq.answer: Web access is useful for competitor research, but early discovery and route generation can work without it.

faq.question: Is it only for products?
faq.answer: No. It can also help name projects, modules, internal tools, and features.

faq.question: What makes it different from a name generator?
faq.answer: It emphasizes positioning, constraints, naming routes, tradeoffs, and validation before final recommendations.
